q: The charge-to-mass ratio (e/m) of an electron was first measured by:
exp: J.J. Thomson determined e/m using cathode ray deflection in electric and magnetic fields.

q: The value of charge of an electron is approximately:
exp: Electron charge is fundamental constant: e = 1.6 × 10^-19 C.

q: The mass of an electron is closest to:
exp: Electron mass is 9.1 × 10^-31 kg.

q: In Thomson’s experiment, cathode rays are deflected by:
exp: Cathode rays respond to both electric and magnetic fields, showing they are charged particles.

q: The nature of cathode rays is:
exp: Cathode rays are streams of electrons emitted from cathode.

q: Which property of electron is independent of the medium?
exp: Charge of electron is invariant, independent of medium or conditions.

q: The radius of circular path of electron in magnetic field increases when:
exp: r = mv/(qB), so radius increases with velocity.

q: Force on moving electron in magnetic field is given by:
exp: Magnetic force depends on velocity, field, and angle.

q: Electron was discovered in which experiment?
exp: J.J. Thomson discovered electron using cathode ray tube.

q: In an electric field, electron moves:
exp: Electron (negative charge) moves opposite to electric field.

q: The specific charge of electron is:
exp: Specific charge is charge per unit mass.

q: Which instrument is used to measure e/m ratio?
exp: Thomson’s cathode ray tube measures e/m.

q: Electron beam in CRT is produced by:
exp: Electrons are emitted from heated cathode (thermionic emission).

q: Velocity selector uses:
exp: Crossed fields allow only specific velocity particles to pass undeflected.

q: In velocity selector, undeflected condition is:
exp: Balance of electric and magnetic forces gives v = E/B.

q: Electron has:
exp: Electron always carries negative charge.

q: Deflection of electron in magnetic field depends on:
exp: Lorentz force depends on velocity and magnetic field.

q: Which is true about electron?
exp: Electron is a fundamental subatomic particle.

q: Electron acceleration in electric field is:
exp: Force qE gives acceleration a = qE/m.

q: In Millikan oil drop experiment, measured quantity is:
exp: Millikan determined fundamental charge e.

q: Electron beam is deflected towards:
exp: Opposite charge attraction causes deflection toward positive plate.

q: Kinetic energy of electron accelerated through potential V is:
exp: Work done by electric field converts to kinetic energy: eV.

q: If potential difference increases, electron speed:
exp: Higher potential gives more kinetic energy.

q: Electron trajectory in uniform magnetic field is:
exp: Magnetic force provides centripetal force leading to circular motion.

q: Radius of electron path is proportional to:
exp: r = mv/(qB), so r ∝ 1/B.

q: Charge of electron was first measured by:
exp: Millikan oil drop experiment measured charge of electron.

q: Electron has wave nature proven by:
exp: Davisson-Germer confirmed electron diffraction.

q: Which particle is used in cathode ray tube?
exp: CRT uses electron beam for image formation.

q: Electron mass compared to proton is:
exp: Electron mass is ~1/1836 of proton mass.

q: In absence of magnetic field, electron moves:
exp: Without magnetic force, motion is straight line in electric field.

q: Force on electron in electric field depends on:
exp: Electric force is F = qE.

q: Electron emission from heated surface is called:
exp: Heat causes electrons to escape from metal surface.

q: Electron behaves as:
exp: Wave-particle duality applies to electrons.

q: Which equation relates momentum of electron to wavelength?
exp: de Broglie relation: wavelength depends on momentum.

q: If velocity of electron doubles, its momentum:
exp: p = mv, so proportional to velocity.

q: Electron is accelerated through 100 V. Its kinetic energy is:
exp: K.E. gained = eV = 100 eV.

q: Which field does NOT affect electron path?
exp: Gravity effect is negligible for electron motion in labs.

q: Electron charge is quantized in units of:
exp: Charge exists in discrete multiples of elementary charge.

q: Electron speed increases when:
exp: Higher accelerating potential increases kinetic energy.

q: Which is correct about electron motion in crossed fields?
opts: Can be undeflected at specific velocity
exp: At v = E/B, forces cancel and motion is straight.

q: Electron was discovered in year:
exp: J.J. Thomson discovered electron in 1897.

q: In cathode ray tube, anode is:
exp: Anode attracts electrons and accelerates them.
